docs/plan.md|P1|[high] integration tests.id: docs/plan.md|P1|[high] integration tests are effectively non-functional: `request()` reads `server.address()` but `server` is never assigned (`test/server.test.js:31`, `test/server.test.js:34`, `test/server.test.js:43`). the file also has conflicting lifecycle setup with two `beforeall` blocks and an extra listener from `createserver(0)` that is never cleanly closed (`test/server.test.js:77`, `test/server.test.js:117`). this undercuts the m6 integration-test goal (`docs/plan.md:69`, `docs/plan.md:105`).
docs/plan.md|P1|[high] integration tests.text: [High] Integration tests are effectively non-functional: `request()` reads `server.address()` but `server` is never assigned (`test/server.test.js:31`, `test/server.test.js:34`, `test/server.test.js:43`). The file also has conflicting lifecycle setup with two `beforeAll` blocks and an extra listener from `createServer(0)` that is never cleanly closed (`test/server.test.js:77`, `test/server.test.js:117`). This undercuts the M6 integration-test goal (`docs/plan.md:69`, `docs/plan.md:105`).

src/server.js|P2|[medium] server constru.id: src/server.js|P2|[medium] server construction is tightly coupled to process lifecycle, which creates architecture/testability risk: `createserver` immediately binds a port and returns `app` instead of a closeable server (`src/server.js:178`, `src/server.js:196`), and installs process-level signal handlers inside the factory (`src/server.js:187`).
src/server.js|P2|[medium] server constru.text: [Medium] Server construction is tightly coupled to process lifecycle, which creates architecture/testability risk: `createServer` immediately binds a port and returns `app` instead of a closeable server (`src/server.js:178`, `src/server.js:196`), and installs process-level signal handlers inside the factory (`src/server.js:187`).

docs/plan.md|P2|[medium] concurrency arc.id: docs/plan.md|P2|[medium] concurrency architecture drift: plan says worker pool replaced `promise.race` (`docs/plan.md:58`), but `extract` command still uses `promise.race` throttling (`src/commands/extract.js:9`, `src/commands/extract.js:23`) while `doc-builder` uses worker pool (`src/lib/doc-builder.js:8`).
docs/plan.md|P2|[medium] concurrency arc.text: [Medium] Concurrency architecture drift: plan says worker pool replaced `Promise.race` (`docs/plan.md:58`), but `extract` command still uses `Promise.race` throttling (`src/commands/extract.js:9`, `src/commands/extract.js:23`) while `doc-builder` uses worker pool (`src/lib/doc-builder.js:8`).

docs/plan.md|P2|`docs/plan.md`: architec.id: docs/plan.md|P2|`docs/plan.md`: architecture and acceptance criteria omit the implemented `/api/parse-file`, `src/lib/temp-store.js`, and `src/lib/output-filename.js`; the plan also claims worker‑pool concurrency replacement, but `src/commands/extract.js` still uses `promise.race`. this makes the plan inaccurate for contributors.

src/server.js|INFO|`src/server.js`: `/ap.id: src/server.js|INFO|`src/server.js`: `/api/parse-file` runs ocr via `execsync` (`pdftoppm`, `tesseract`) on the request thread with no dependency checks or upload size limits. this can block the server and will fail at runtime if those binaries aren’t installed.
src/server.js|INFO|`src/server.js`: `/ap.text: `src/server.js`: `/api/parse-file` runs OCR via `execSync` (`pdftoppm`, `tesseract`) on the request thread with no dependency checks or upload size limits. This can block the server and will fail at runtime if those binaries aren’t installed.

src/lib/content-extractor.js|INFO|anti‑b.id: src/lib/content-extractor.js|INFO|anti‑bot detection isn’t applied at all tiers: `src/lib/content-extractor.js` only checks `isrealcontent` for direct http fetches; puppeteer/scrapeowl html is accepted without challenge detection. this contradicts the plan and risks compiling interstitial pages as content.
src/lib/content-extractor.js|INFO|anti‑b.text: Anti‑bot detection isn’t applied at all tiers: `src/lib/content-extractor.js` only checks `isRealContent` for direct HTTP fetches; Puppeteer/ScrapeOWL HTML is accepted without challenge detection. This contradicts the plan and risks compiling interstitial pages as content.
